current_storage: Git-tracked Foundry docs/evidence artifacts plus local excluded/readiness output
observations: Foundry has a large tracked evidence corpus with repeated campaign readbacks and run links
Foundry scripts already emit digest-bound local artifacts and enforce public-safe relative paths
release and upload surfaces exist in the repository but are outside this node and remain prohibited
externalization without preserving digest, schema, replay, and no-promotion flags would weaken auditability

proposed_catalog_record.public_fixture_policy: retain small contract fixtures, readiness gates, negative controls, and safety assertions in Git
proposed_catalog_record.bulk_artifact_policy: move historical campaign outputs to an operator-approved content-addressed catalog
proposed_catalog_record.dual_read_policy: Foundry local readback and catalog readback must agree before retirement
proposed_catalog_record.deletion_policy: no deletion or upload is authorized by this node

staged_plan.gate: classify tracked evidence by contract, campaign, and replayability
define owner, digest, replay, retention, and visibility fields
compare local and catalog readbacks without changing source artifacts
separate explicit authorization before external storage or upload
only after rollback and replay evidence is complete
